rulings.Case Title: FINAS BELK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Goodrich Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant fails to sustain its burden of production. Defendant fails to present admissible affirmative evidence negating an element of the plaintiff's cause of action. Defendant also fails to present evidence demonstrating that the plaintiff does not have and cannot obtain needed evidence. Plaintiff's objections to the McCrillis affidavit are sustained. Mr. McCrillis fails to provide sufficient foundation for his statements. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALMER VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carlisle Corp.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Carlisle Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant fails to sustain its burden of production. Defendant overstates Dicola's holding and applicability to the instant action. Mr. Palmer is testifying to his personal knowledge as it relates to his work with Carlisle brake blocks from 1962 to 1969. Unlike the plaintiff in Dicola, Mr. Palmer worked with the defendant's brake blocks "regularly" and his description of the packaging in question provides circumstantial evidence of its manufacturer and/or distributor. =(220/HEK)
